data.output:
cli/cmd/agent_check.go
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

const checkProbeTimeout = 5 * time.Second

type checkResultEntry struct {
	Provider  string `json:"provider"`
	Harness   string `json:"harness"`
	Status    string `json:"status"`
	LatencyMs int64  `json:"latency_ms"`
	Error     string `json:"error"`
}

func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "check",
		Short: "Probe provider runtime availability (liveness, model inventory)",
		Long: `Probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint to report runtime availability.

Semantic distinction from 'doctor': doctor answers "is my config valid?" (config
validation, missing API keys); check answers "what providers can I use right now?"
(runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are available).

Exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model.
Exits 1 otherwise.`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			svc, err := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
			if err != nil {
				return fmt.Errorf("loading agent config: %w", err)
			}

			providerName, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
			asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), checkProbeTimeout*2)
			defer cancel()

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks a bead that is not eligible for agent dispatch —
65		// either decomposed into child beads by the triage gate or parked for
66		// human intervention after a triage-overflow event. Blocked beads are
67		// excluded from ReadyExecution and will not be auto-dispatched.
68		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
69		// StatusProposed marks an operator-prompt bead awaiting approval. It is
70		// excluded from execute-loop drain until transitioned to open via the
71		// approval flow, or to cancelled when the operator declines it.
72		StatusProposed = "proposed"
73		// StatusCancelled marks a bead that was rejected before any execution
74		// took place (e.g. an operator-prompt bead the user chose not to run).
75		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
76	)
77	
78	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
79	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in

data.output:
/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx
ID:       ddx-a946c744
Title:    ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:checks, kind:platform, prevention, triage:needs-investigation
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract
Accept:   1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.

That mixes design, implementation, integration, and e2e across unrelated subsystems and is well over the ~6-AC / ~500-line / ~5-file threshold.   end-to-end fixtures belong to ddx-3e60fd84.
- Implementing the parent here would duplicate or pre-empt the decomposed
  children, creating merge conflicts when those beads execute and violating the
  one-bead-one-commit boundary the queue relies on.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
- Either (a) close ddx-a946c744 administratively as superseded by its children
  (preferred — the decomposition note is the contract), or
- (b) if the parent should still be executed monolithically, remove the
  decomposition note and cancel/close the two child beads first so this attempt
  is not racing them.

Recommended action: close ddx-a946c744 as superseded; let ddx-aee651ec then
ddx-3e60fd84 execute in dependency order.
rationale: Bead ddx-a946c744 — no changes this attempt.

What is done:
- Reviewed the bead description, acceptance criteria, and notes.

What is blocking:
- The bead's own <notes> field declares it has been decomposed:
  "decomposed into ddx-aee651ec (protocol package + ddx ac CLI) and

   ddx-3e60fd84 depends on ddx-aee651ec."
- The full AC set (1-10) spans both child beads: protocol/loader/runner/ddx ac CLI
  belong to ddx-aee651ec; pre_merge integration, checks_bypass, and the

- Implementing the parent here would duplicate or pre-empt the decomposed

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestAgentCheckSuccess(t *testing.T) {
	srv := newOAIModelsStub(t, []string{"fast-model", "slow-model"})
	dir := makeProviderTestDir(t, oaiAgentConfig(srv.URL+"/v1", "fast-model"))

	out, err := executeCommand(
		NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand(),
		"agent", "check",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "testprovider")
	require.Contains(t, out, "OK")
}

func TestAgentCheckAnthropicWithKey(t *testing.T) {
	cfg := `providers:
  claude:
    type: anthropic
    api_key: test-api-key-xyz
default: claude
`
	dir := makeProviderTestDir(t, cfg)

	out, err := executeCommand(
		NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand(),
		"agent", "check",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "claude")
	require.Contains(t, out, "OK")
}

// dead provider but exits without error because at least one provider is OK.
// Using two providers avoids triggering os.Exit(1).
func TestAgentCheckMixedReachability(t *testing.T) {
	live := newOAIModelsStub(t, []string{"good-model"})
	dead := newOAIModelsStub(t, nil)
	deadURL := dead.URL
	dead.Close()

data.text: Now I have enough. Let me write the test:

input.content:
package cmd

import (
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// initRepoWithBead creates a fresh git repo with one committed file, an
// initialized .ddx bead store containing one bead, and (optionally) writes
// the given check YAML under .ddx/checks/.
func initRepoWithBead(t *testing.T, beadID string, labels []string, checkYAML string) string {
	t.Helper()
	root := t.TempDir()

	gitInit := func(args ...string) {
		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", root}, args...)...)
		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
	}
	gitInit("init", "-q", "-b", "main")
	gitInit("config", "user.email", "t@example.com")
	gitInit("config", "user.name", "Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	gitInit("add", ".")
	gitInit("commit", "-q", "-m", "init")

	ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "ac test", Labels: labels}))

	if checkYAML != "" {
		checksDir := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "checks")
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(checksDir, 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "fixture.yaml"), []byte(checkYAML), 0o644))
	}

	return root
}

func TestAcRun_PassExitsZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-pass"
	yaml := `
name: passer
command: 'printf ''{"status":"pass","message":"ok"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[PASS] passer")
}

func TestAcRun_BlockExitsNonZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-block"
	yaml := `
name: blocker
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block","message":"nope"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[BLOCK] blocker")
}

func TestAcRun_ErrorOnMissingResultFile(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-err"
	yaml := `
name: silent
command: 'true'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[ERROR] silent")
}

func TestAcRun_AppliesToFiltersByLabel(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-filter"
	yaml := `
name: skipped
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
applies_to:
  labels: [area:other]
`
	// Bead has label area:foo, check filters on area:other → should be skipped.
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, []string{"area:foo"}, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err, "filter should skip the only check, leaving zero failures")
	require.NotContains(t, out, "skipped")
	// Header line is still printed.
	require.Contains(t, out, "ac run")
}

func TestAcRun_OnlyFlag(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-only"
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, "")
	checksDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "checks")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(checksDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "a.yaml"), []byte(`
name: alpha
command: 'printf ''{"status":"pass"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "b.yaml"), []byte(`
name: beta
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`), 0o644))

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(),
		"ac", "run", beadID, "--check", "alpha")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[PASS] alpha")
	require.NotContains(t, out, "beta")
}

func TestAcRun_UnknownBeadFails(t *testing.T) {
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, "ddx-ac-known", nil, "")
	_, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(),
		"ac", "run", "ddx-does-not-exist")
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.True(t, strings.Contains(err.Error(), "load bead") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found"),
		"unexpected error: %v", err)
}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(checks): protocol package + ddx ac CLI for pre-merge checks [ddx-aee651ec]

Add language-agnostic check protocol so projects can declare mechanical
pre-merge gates in .ddx/checks/*.yaml. This slice ships:

- internal/checks/protocol.go: Check, AppliesTo, Result, Violation, Status
  (pass|block|error), InvocationContext; full schema + I/O contract in
  package doc.
- internal/checks/loader.go: LoadDir/LoadFile read .ddx/checks/*.yaml with
  KnownFields(true); reject malformed YAML, unknown fields, missing
  required fields, and duplicate names with file:line context.
- internal/checks/runner.go: parallel sh -c execution with documented env
  injection (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR,
  RUN_ID, CHECK_NAME); reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; non-zero exit,
  missing file, invalid JSON, and unknown status all map to status=error.
- cmd/ac.go: `ddx ac run <bead-id>` resolves bead, computes DIFF_BASE
  (--from > bead.Extra base_rev > merge-base with main) and DIFF_HEAD,
  loads checks, writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<runID>/, prints
  per-check status with violations, exits non-zero on block/error.
  Supports --check <name> for targeted iteration.

NO integration with execute-bead loop yet — that is sibling slice
ddx-3e60fd84.
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. All ACs satisfied:

1. ✅ `cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go` exist with documented types.
2. ✅ Loader rejects malformed YAML, unknown fields, missing required fields with file:line via `KnownFields(true)` and wrapped errors.
3. ✅ Runner injects all documented env vars, runs checks in parallel, treats non-zero exit / missing result file / invalid JSON as `status=error`.
4. ✅ `ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] [--from <rev>]` runs configured checks, prints summary, exits non-zero on block/error.
5. ✅ `go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/...` green for all new tests (`TestRun_*`, `TestLoadDir_*`, `TestLoadFile_*`, `TestApplies`, `TestAcRun_*`); pre-existing unrelated failures (installation/review tests) confirmed not introduced by this change.
6. ✅ lefthook pre-commit (with CGO_ENABLED=1 for `go test -race`) passes.
